content.problemStatement:
**This is the easy version of the problem. In this version, you may find any permutation that satisfies the conditions.

This problem is worth 100100100 points in divisions 333 and 444, and 606060 points in divisions 111 and 222.**

You are given an integer NNN (N‚Č•3N\geq 3N‚Č•3).

Find a permutation‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† PPP of the integers 111 to NNN such that Pi‚Č†iP_i \neq iPi‚ÄčÓÄ†=i for every index iii, and

max‚Ā°i=1N(Pi‚äēi)\max_{i=1}^N \left( P_i \oplus i\right)i=1maxN‚Äč(Pi‚Äč‚äēi)is minimized.

Here, ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes the bitwise XOR operation.

If there are multiple permutations that minimize this quantity, you may print any of them.

‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä†A permutation of the integers 111 to NNN is an array of length NNN that contains every integer from 111 to NNN.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN space-separated integers denoting the permutation you found.

If there are multiple possible answers, you may print any of them.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 5‚čÖ1055\cdot 10^55‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: For P=[3,1,2]P = [3, 1, 2]P=[3,1,2], we obtain:

P1‚äē1=3‚äē1=2P_1\oplus 1 = 3\oplus 1 = 2P1‚Äč‚äē1=3‚äē1=2
P2‚äē2=1‚äē2=3P_2\oplus 2 = 1\oplus 2 = 3P2‚Äč‚äē2=1‚äē2=3
P3‚äē3=2‚äē3=1P_3\oplus 3 = 2\oplus 3 = 1P3‚Äč‚äē3=2‚äē3=1

The maximum among these is 333.
It can be proved that no permutation of length 333 has Pi‚Č†iP_i \neq iPi‚ÄčÓÄ†=i and Pi‚äēi<3P_i\oplus i \lt 3Pi‚Äč‚äēi<3 for every index iii.
Test case 222: The minimum possible maximum XOR is 555.
